title: Buttery Crunchy Polish Cornmeal Flatbread
description: A crisp, buttery cornmeal flatbread inspired by Polish flavors, perfect for easing a hangover and crafted without added sugar.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 °F (220 °C) and line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a small saucepan over medium heat, melt the butter. Add salt, pepper, garlic powder, oregano, and smoked paprika; stir until fragrant (about 30 s).
Stir in the cornmeal and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes until it begins to toast and a dough forms.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the dough cool for 5 minutes. Then roll the dough on a lightly cornmeal‑dusted surface into circles about 1/4‑inch thick.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat. Place a dough circle in the skillet and cook 2–3 minutes on each side until golden brown and crisp. Transfer to the prepared baking sheet.
Repeat with the remaining dough circles.
While the skillet is still hot, pour in the broth, scraping up any browned bits. Let it simmer for 1–2 minutes until reduced into a light glaze.
Drizzle the reduced broth glaze over the flatbread on the sheet. Sprinkle grated Parmesan and lemon zest over the tops.
Return the sheet to the oven and bake for 5 minutes to melt the cheese and finish crisping the edges.
Remove from oven, garnish with chopped parsley, and serve warm.
